description: Introduce one-level organization hierarchy (parent/child), multi-org user access with inherited access through the hierarchy, contact-person-per-elevator, and a refactored Excel import that drops hard-coded sheet names, adds explicit sheet + org-name mapping steps, and removes emergency-phone / removed-elevator imports.

userStories.title: Add parentId to organizations with one-level-deep constraint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want organizations to support a self-referential parent so we can model a one-level parent/child hierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: Add nullable `parentId` column to `organizations` table referencing `organizations.id`

Enforce one-level-deep constraint at the database layer: an org with a non-null `parentId` cannot itself be referenced by another org's `parentId` (check constraint, trigger, or validation in the write path — pick the simplest reliable option)
Drizzle schema updated with self-relation
Migration generated and runs cleanly against local Postgres
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add user-organization many-to-many join table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a join table between users and organizations so a user can have direct access to multiple orgs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `userOrganizations` table (userId, organizationId, createdAt, composite PK on (userId, organizationId))
Remove `organizationId` column from `users` table
Drizzle schema + relations updated (user → userOrganizations → organization)
Cascade delete configured on both userId and organizationId
Migration runs cleanly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Move contact person from organization to elevator
userStories.description: As a user, I want each elevator to have its own contact person so different buildings can have different on-site contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove `contactPerson*` fields from `organizations` table
Add `contactPersonName`, `contactPersonPhone`, `contactPersonEmail` columns to `elevators` table (single contact per elevator)
Drizzle schema updated
Migration runs cleanly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Clear existing data reset script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all existing elevators, organizations, user-org links, and related records cleared so the new schema starts with a clean slate.
acceptanceCriteria: Reset script truncates relevant tables in correct FK order
Script is guarded to prevent accidental execution against prod (e.g. requires an explicit --confirm flag or checks NODE_ENV / DATABASE_URL)
Script lives under `scripts/` or `packages/db/` and is runnable via pnpm
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Effective organization access helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a helper that returns the full set of org IDs a user has access to (direct grants + direct children of those grants) so client-app queries can filter correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Helper function `getEffectiveOrganizationIds(userId)` in `packages/auth/` or `packages/db/`
Uses a single join (no recursion — hierarchy is exactly one level deep)
Returns deduplicated array of org IDs: direct grants ∪ orgs where `parentId` is in the direct grants
Unit tests cover: user with a single direct grant (no children), user with a direct grant that has children, user with multiple direct grants at different roots
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Admin can set parent on organization
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to set a parent organization when creating or editing an org so I can build the one-level hierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: Org create/edit form includes a searchable 'Parent organization' dropdown
Dropdown only shows root orgs (parentId = null) — orgs that already have a parent are not valid parent choices
Dropdown excludes the org being edited
If the org being edited already has children, the parent dropdown is disabled with a tooltip ('This organization has sub-organizations and cannot itself have a parent')
Clearing the dropdown sets `parentId` to null (makes it a top-level org)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Admin can grant user access to multiple orgs
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to grant a user access to one or more organizations so multi-org users have a single login.
acceptanceCriteria: User edit page shows list of orgs the user currently has direct access to
Admin can add/remove orgs from the user's access list
UI visually distinguishes inherited access (via parent grant) from direct grants — icon or text label, not color alone
UI prevents creating a direct grant on a user for both a parent and one of its children (redundant — the child grant is implied)
When revoking a direct grant, a toast confirms and lists the child orgs the user also lost implicit access to
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Warn when org parent change affects user access
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to know when changing an org's parent will grant or revoke inherited access for existing users so I don't silently expand or cut off access.
acceptanceCriteria: On save of a `parentId` change, compute the diff of effective access for users with a direct grant on the old parent and/or the new parent
If any user gains or loses inherited access, show a warning dialog with aggregate counts ('X users will gain access, Y users will lose access')
Dialog includes an expandable 'View affected users' section listing name, email, and gain/lose indicator
Admin must confirm explicitly before the change is applied
No warning when the change has no access-inheritance impact
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Effective access preview in user-org management
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see the total effective org count and which orgs are inherited vs direct for a user so I understand the reach of each grant.
acceptanceCriteria: User access page shows 'Effective access: N organizations' total
Each direct grant row is expandable to show the child orgs it transitively grants
Inherited orgs shown read-only, labeled 'via [parent name]'
Direct grants use a solid badge; inherited shown with a muted/outline badge + label (never color alone)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Scope client-app queries to current parent context
userStories.description: As a client-app user, I want every page to show data for the parent organization I'm currently viewing (including its direct children) so each screen has a clear, single-context scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Client app routes include current parent org in the URL: `/:parentOrgId/...`
Every client-app server function filters by `organizationId IN (currentParentId, ...childrenOfCurrentParent)`
Middleware validates the URL's `parentOrgId` is in the user's effective direct grants — otherwise redirect/404
`context.user.organizationId` is replaced with `context.currentContextOrgIds` (array: parent + direct children)
Invalid or inaccessible `:parentOrgId` redirects to last-used or default context with a toast ('That organization is unavailable')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — a user with access to a parent org sees elevators from its child orgs merged into each view

userStories.title: Client-app parent context header switcher
userStories.description: As a client-app user with one or more parent orgs, I want the app to place me in one parent context at a time with a switcher in the header if I have more than one.
acceptanceCriteria: On login, user lands on last-used parent context (via cookie/localStorage). Fallback: first direct grant alphabetically
Header shows the current parent org name
If user has 2+ direct grants, the header name is a dropdown listing those direct grants (not their children); selecting one routes to that parent
If user has only 1 direct grant, no switcher is shown (name is static text)
A user with a direct grant on a child org only (not its parent) treats that child as their context, as if it were a root
Switching updates the URL (`/:parentOrgId/...`) and replaces list/dashboard state (filters, drill-downs, pagination reset)
Long org names truncate with ellipsis; full name available via accessible tooltip and `aria-label`
Trigger button has `aria-haspopup='menu'`, `aria-expanded`, and `aria-label` describing state
Dropdown uses semantic menu roles; current selection marked `aria-current='true'` and a visible checkmark (not color alone)
Dropdown is keyboard-navigable (arrows, Enter, Escape); closes on outside click; focus returns to trigger on close
When user has 10+ direct grants, dropdown includes a type-to-filter input at the top
While orgs are loading on initial app load, header shows a skeleton placeholder for the switcher slot
Narrow viewport (<768px): switcher opens as a full-screen or bottom sheet rather than a dropdown
After switch, change announced via `aria-live='polite'`, focus moves to the main content heading, and content crossfades (≤200ms, respects `prefers-reduced-motion`)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Sub-org filter on client-app list pages
userStories.description: As a client-app user in a parent context that has sub-orgs, I want to filter list pages by sub-org and see which sub-org each row belongs to so I can narrow focus when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: When current parent has 1+ children, list pages show a sub-org filter and a sub-org column
When current parent has zero children, the filter and column are hidden entirely
Filter is single-select; default option is 'All sub-organizations'
When a specific sub-org is selected, a dismissable filter chip appears above the table ('Sub-Org A ×') mirroring existing filter-chip treatment
Filter state persists in URL query params (consistent with existing URL-driven filter pattern)
Empty state when the selected sub-org has zero matching rows: 'No elevators in [Sub-Org Name]' with a 'Clear filter' action
Narrow viewport (<768px): sub-org filter moves into the existing filter drawer; sub-org column is hidden (sub-org name appears inline in row's secondary text instead)
Filter change announces the new result count via `aria-live='polite'`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard aggregates across current parent context
userStories.description: As a client-app user, I want the dashboard to show aggregated numbers across the current parent org and all its sub-orgs so I see the full picture at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard queries use `currentContextOrgIds` (parent + direct children)
No sub-org filter shown on the dashboard by default
Existing chart drill-down mechanism can reveal per-sub-org breakdowns where supported
Each chart loads independently — one slow or failed query does not block the others
Each chart has its own skeleton placeholder during load (no blank axis frames)
Each chart has its own error state with a retry action; never a broken axis or blank panel
Empty-data state: each chart shows a 'No data yet' message rather than an empty axis
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Modernisering page sub-org drill-down
userStories.description: As a client-app user viewing modernisering charts for a parent context with sub-orgs, I want to drill down to a specific sub-org via the existing chart-click mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: When current parent has children, a chart dimension (or existing clickable bar) supports drilling down by sub-org
Clicking a sub-org in a chart filters the other charts on the page to that sub-org via URL params
Clickable bars have visible hover/focus state (cursor pointer + outline or elevation change)
Chart bars are keyboard-focusable; Enter or Space triggers drill-down
After drill-down, a dismissable filter chip appears above the chart grid ('Sub-Org A ×') — clearing restores aggregated view
Drill-down change announced via `aria-live='polite'`
When parent has no children, no sub-org drill-down affordance is rendered at all
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Drop hard-coded sheet name validation in Excel parser
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the parser to stop requiring specific sheet names (`Hissar`, `Nödtelefoner`, `Rivna hissar`) so files with any sheet structure can be imported.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the mandatory-sheet check in `parse-excel-import-with-mapping.ts`
Parser can open any workbook and return metadata about each sheet (name, row count, first-row preview)
Existing callers still compile
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove emergency phone and removed-elevator import
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to drop the emergency phone (`Nödtelefoner`) and removed elevator (`Rivna hissar`) import paths so the codebase only handles elevators.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete the hard-coded parsers for `Nödtelefoner` and `Rivna hissar`
Remove emergency-phone-specific header aliases and types
Remove the join logic that merges emergency phone data into elevators
Any UI elements related to these two flows are removed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sheet selection step in import
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to pick which sheets to import from after uploading the file so I can skip sheets that aren't relevant.
acceptanceCriteria: After upload, a step shows a list of all sheets in the workbook
Each sheet row shows sheet name, row count, and a preview of the first row's values
Admin selects sheets via checkboxes (default: all selected)
At least one sheet must be selected to proceed
Sheets with only a header row (zero data rows) show an inline warning
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Parse Excel and extract distinct org names
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the import to parse the Excel file and return distinct org names (case-sensitive) so the mapping UI can drive from a clean dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: Server function returns `{ orgNames: string[], rowCount: number, parsedRows: ... }` from uploaded Excel
Names are deduplicated as exact strings (case preserved — 'Bostadsbolaget' and 'bostadsbolaget' appear as two separate entries)
Parsed row data held server-side (or re-uploaded on import) so mapping UI doesn't have to re-parse
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Per-sheet column mapping with inherited defaults
userStories.description: As an admin, I want each selected sheet to go through its own column-mapping step but with the previous sheet's mapping carried over as a default so I don't redo identical work.
acceptanceCriteria: After sheet selection, the column-mapping step runs once per selected sheet sequentially, with a visible progress indicator ('Sheet 2 of 4: [sheet name]')
First sheet uses existing auto-mapping logic (via `HEADER_ALIASES`)
Subsequent sheets inherit the previous sheet's resolved mapping as their starting default, overlaid on auto-detection
Admin can change any mapping on any sheet before advancing
Mandatory field validation applies per sheet before advancing (blocks Next until all mandatory fields mapped)
Missing mandatory fields shown prominently (red badge + list)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Merge rows from all selected sheets before org mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I want rows from every selected sheet merged into a single parsed dataset before the org-mapping step so the downstream flow is sheet-count-agnostic.
acceptanceCriteria: After column mapping is resolved for all selected sheets, rows are concatenated into one dataset
`_source_sheet` metadata is preserved on each row for debug/trace
The org-name-mapping step receives the deduplicated org names across all merged rows
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build the import org-name mapping UI
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a mapping screen showing every org name in the Excel alongside a dropdown of existing orgs, with auto-selected exact matches, so I can confirm or change each mapping before running the import.
acceptanceCriteria: Table UI: one row per distinct org name in Excel
Each row has a searchable (type-to-filter) dropdown of existing orgs with keyboard navigation
Exact case-sensitive matches are auto-selected (shown as pre-selected, not silently applied)
Rows with no match show a 'Create organization' option in the dropdown
Admin can change any auto-selected mapping
Import button is disabled until every row has a resolved org ID
If no matching orgs exist at all, step still works — every row defaults to 'Create organization' with a helpful banner
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Inline 'Create organization' dialog during import
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to create a new organization from within the import flow without losing my parsed Excel or partial mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialog opens from the 'Create organization' option in the mapping dropdown
Minimal fields: name (prefilled from Excel row), optional parent organization
On save, the new org is created and auto-selected for that mapping row
Parsed Excel and other mapping selections persist across dialog open/close
Dialog confirms discard when closed with modified fields
Dialog traps focus and restores focus to the triggering control on close
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Run the import with resolved mapping
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the import to create elevators (not organizations) using the confirmed mapping so each elevator is linked to the right org.
acceptanceCriteria: Import creates one elevator per merged Excel row
`organizationId` is set from the resolved mapping
`contactPersonName`, `contactPersonPhone`, `contactPersonEmail` are populated from the Excel row
No organizations are created as a side effect of running the import
Import is transactional — all-or-nothing, failure rolls back
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Import flow stepper with back navigation
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see which step I'm on during the import and be able to go back without losing my work.
acceptanceCriteria: Step indicator visible on every import screen ('Step 2 of 5: Select sheets')
Steps: (1) Upload, (2) Select sheets, (3) Map columns, (4) Map org names, (5) Review & import
Current step highlighted; completed steps marked done; future steps disabled/muted
Back button on every step except upload
Going back preserves all state — uploaded file, sheet selection, column mappings, org-name mappings
Re-entering a later step after going back does not reset its state
Each step has exactly one primary CTA (Next / Import) and at most one secondary action (Back / Cancel)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: File upload validation and error states
userStories.description: As an admin, I want clear error messages when an upload fails so I know how to fix the problem rather than the flow silently breaking.
acceptanceCriteria: Reject non-Excel files (not `.xlsx` / `.xls`) with a message stating the accepted formats
Enforce a file size limit (10MB) with a message stating the limit and the uploaded size
Surface a parse error if the workbook is corrupt or unreadable
Surface an error if the workbook has zero sheets
Every error message states cause AND recovery step (e.g. 'File must be .xlsx — re-export from Excel and try again')
Errors are announced via `aria-live='assertive'` for screen readers
Upload zone supports drag-drop with visible hover state plus a keyboard-accessible 'Choose file' button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Empty and unmappable sheet handling
userStories.description: As an admin, I want meaningful feedback when selected sheets have no usable data or missing mandatory columns so I'm never stuck at a dead end.
acceptanceCriteria: Sheets with only a header row (zero data rows) show an inline warning on the sheet-selection row
If every selected sheet has zero data rows, the 'Next' button is disabled with an explanation
Column mapping surfaces missing mandatory fields prominently (red badge + list)
'Next' from column mapping is disabled until every mandatory field is mapped (per sheet when headers differ; once when headers match)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Confirm navigation away from unsaved import
userStories.description: As an admin, I don't want to accidentally lose my parsed Excel and in-progress mappings by clicking away or refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating away (route change, tab close, refresh) after upload triggers a confirmation dialog
Dialog states clearly that import progress will be lost
Confirmation only fires when in-progress state exists (not on bare upload step before a file is selected, not after successful import)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Import execution progress and result reporting
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see progress during import execution and a clear result afterwards so large imports don't feel like a black box and failures are actionable.
acceptanceCriteria: During import, show a progress indicator with batch progress (e.g. 'Importing… 250 / 1200 elevators')
Import button disabled during execution
On success: summary screen with counts per org ('Created 200 elevators in Bostadsbolaget, 80 in Sub-Org A')
On failure: error screen stating which row/sheet/field caused the failure + reminder that the transaction was rolled back, plus 'Back to mapping' and 'Start over' actions
Parse and analyze steps each show skeleton/loading feedback when they exceed 300ms
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Accessibility sweep across import and org management flows
userStories.description: As a keyboard or screen-reader user, I want the entire import flow and org management pages to be fully operable with predictable focus and clear announcements.
acceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements keyboard-reachable in logical tab order
On step advance in the import flow, focus moves to the step heading
Form errors use `aria-live='polite'`; hard failures use `role='alert'`
All icon-only buttons have `aria-label`
Modal dialogs (create-org, change-parent warning) trap focus and restore focus on close
Color is never the sole indicator (inherited vs direct access, valid vs invalid mapping, required vs optional column — always icon or text too)
Color contrast ≥4.5:1 for all text in both light and dark mode
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
